category_title: Students with internalizing behaviors and low self-confidence
minimal_context: The student is asked to bring their completed example to the front of the classroom.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please come up to the front of the class to show your example.

STUDENT: (Stays in seat and looks down) I don't want to go to the front.

TUTOR: Just bring your paper up here.

STUDENT: (Slowly walks up to the front of the class to show the example, keeping head down)

TUTOR: You finished the task. Well done.

STUDENT: (Pulls the paper away and shrinks back, visibly distressed) No it isn't, it's all wrong.

options.A: Assign the student to sit in the front desks during classroom activities.
options.B: Implement classroom activities with less demanding rules during the learning sessions.
options.C: Subdivide lessons into 15-minute segments and introduce short breaks between learning activities.
options.D: Establish goals through a joint process between the student and teacher based on assessment results.
